hints.transcribeVoiceMemo: Transcribe a {file:voice memo} and turn it into {source:Notion} tasks
menu.resetToDefaultsDetail:
To reset Craft Agent to defaults, quit the app and run:

bun run fresh-start

This will delete all configuration, credentials, workspaces, and sessions.
mode.exploreFullDesc: Read-only exploration. Blocks writes, never prompts.

settings.messaging.bindings.title: Active Bindings
settings.messaging.lark.instructions:
1. Open Lark Open Platform (lark) or Feishu Open Platform (feishu) and create a Custom App
2. Copy the App ID (cli_…) and App Secret
3. Enable im:message + im:message.group_at_msg + im:message:send_as_bot scopes
4. Subscribe to the im.message.receive_v1 event in long connection mode
5. Leave the Encrypt Key field blank — long connection mode doesn't use payload encryption
settings.messaging.lark.title: Lark / Feishu
settings.messaging.telegram.access.allow: No one can use this bot yet — pair from Telegram or accept a pending request.
Not enforced — bot is publicly accessible.
settings.messaging.telegram.access.banne: Anyone with the bot username can DM it and start a new session in your workspace, or list and bind to your existing sessions.
Some bindings are still publicly accessible. Click Lock down again to close them.
settings.messaging.telegram.access.bindi: Only the explicitly checked users can use this binding.
All workspace owners can use this binding.
Anyone in an accepted chat can use this binding. Use only for public-facing bots.
No known users. Add owners from the workspace allow-list above first.
settings.messaging.telegram.access.owner: No allowed users yet. Send /pair from your Telegram account to add yourself, or wait for a pending request below.
settings.messaging.telegram.access.pendi: 1 sender was rejected — review to allow.
{{count}} senders were rejected — review to allow.
settings.messaging.telegram.instructions:
1. Open Telegram and search for @BotFather
2. Send /newbot and follow the prompts
3. Copy the token and paste it here
settings.messaging.telegram.supergroup.a: Automations with a 'telegramTopic' set will post into matching topics here (created on first use).
settings.messaging.telegram.supergroup.d: Add the bot to your supergroup, then type the command in any topic. The bot needs privacy mode disabled (BotFather → /setprivacy → Disable) or admin rights to read non-command messages.
Send this command from any topic in your supergroup. The dialog closes once paired.

settings.permissions.aboutText1: Permissions control how much autonomy your agent has. In Explore mode, the agent can only read and research — perfect for understanding a problem before committing to changes. When you're ready, switch to Execute mode to let the agent implement the plan autonomously.
settings.permissions.aboutText2: A good workflow: start in Explore to let the agent investigate, review the proposed plan, then execute with confidence.
settings.permissions.defaultPermissionsD: App-level patterns allowed in Explore mode. Commands not on this list are blocked.
settings.permissions.noCustomPermissions: Create a permissions.json file in your workspace to add custom rules.
settings.permissions.title: Permissions
settings.permissions.workspaceCustomizat: Workspace-level patterns that extend the app defaults above.
settings.piExtensions.delegatePromptAuto: When enabled, automation prompt triggers are delegated to the pi prompt-automation extension. When disabled, craft's PromptHandler creates the session as usual.
